focus: Official Aside CLI and MCP surface
noveltyJustification: Eight independently cited findings establish the real CLI, stdio MCP transport, single repl tool, browser-profile prerequisite, and account/permission boundary; only browser-bound execution remains untested.
keyQuestions: What is the complete stable Aside CLI command and option surface for browser automation?
What exact MCP tools and schemas does aside mcp expose, and what is the session/daemon lifecycle?
Which auth, privacy, permission, and unattended-automation boundaries must mcp-aside-devtools document?
answeredQuestions: Aside ships a standalone CLI with account, exec, repl, and mcp subcommands.
aside mcp is a local stdio MCP server whose live inventory contains one repl tool.
A fresh aside mcp process requires an Aside browser-profile/task binding before browser operations.
ruledOut: Separate Aside MCP tools named navigate, dom, screenshot, console, or network
Documented remote HTTP/SSE MCP endpoint or static bearer-token configuration
Treating a fresh aside mcp process as sufficient to control any browser

label: Standalone aside CLI and subcommands verified
Official curl installer and macOS paths verified
aside mcp is local stdio
source: local: aside mcp --help and MCP initialize

label: Live MCP inventory contains one repl tool
Browser operations require a bound Aside profile

reason: no official or local transport surface found
